title: ConvertKit vs AWeber (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare ConvertKit vs AWeber: pricing, features, ease of use, and which email marketing platform suits your needs.
h1: ConvertKit vs AWeber: Detailed Comparison for Creators and Solopreneurs
intro: ConvertKit and AWeber are both popular freemium email marketing platforms designed to help creators, bloggers, and solopreneurs build and monetize their audiences. While they share similar pricing models and core features, they differ significantly in focus, feature depth, and user experience. This comparison will help you determine which platform aligns best with your business goals.
choose_a_if: You sell digital products, courses, or physical goods and need built-in commerce features
You want advanced automation workflows with a visual builder for complex sequences
You're willing to invest more for a platform specifically designed for creators and higher G2 ratings (4.4)
choose_b_if: You're budget-conscious and want the lowest entry price at $15/month
You prefer simplicity and are new to email marketing
You prioritize Trustpilot ratings and customer satisfaction (4.0 vs 3.2)

faq.a: ConvertKit is better for creators focused on monetization, with superior automation, commerce features, and higher G2 ratings. However, 'better' depends on your needs—AWeber may be preferable if you prioritize affordability and simplicity over advanced features.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, ConvertKit or AWeber?
faq.a: AWeber is cheaper, starting at $15/month compared to ConvertKit's $29/month. However, ConvertKit offers a larger free tier (1,000 vs 500 subscribers), which extends the time you can use the platform without paying.

faq.q: Can I switch from AWeber to ConvertKit?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms support subscriber list imports and exports. You can export your contacts from AWeber and import them into ConvertKit relatively easily. Most email marketing platforms are designed to facilitate customer migration.
